name: Mechanism stabilising the EW vacuum against the SM near-criticality
closability_note: The SM with measured (m_t, m_H, alpha_s) sits in a metastable vacuum with tunneling lifetime ~10^600 years — much longer than the universe's age, so the metastability is not phenomenologically alarming. The puzzle is the apparent fine-tuning that puts us SO close to the stability boundary in the (m_t, m_H) plane. Closability is contested: anthropic readings (the boundary is selected because near-criticality is required for some other physics, like inflation or dark energy) are non-predictive; structural readings (a UV mechanism flattens the Higgs potential at the Planck scale, putting us at the boundary by RG flow) require very specific assumptions. The puzzle could also dissolve if m_t measurements shift — current top-mass uncertainty straddles the stability/metastability boundary.
summary: The SM Higgs quartic lambda runs to a small negative value near the Planck scale; the EW vacuum is metastable but extremely long-lived. No framework predicts WHY we sit so close to the stability boundary.
what_closes_it: A mechanism that either (a) modifies the Higgs potential at high scales to push lambda(M_Pl) to exactly the stability boundary (requiring new physics that flattens the quartic running, e.g. a heavy scalar that contributes positively to beta_lambda), (b) provides a structural reason for near-criticality (e.g. critical-Higgs inflation, where the inflationary observables require lambda(M_Pl) ~ 0; Bezrukov-Shaposhnikov), or (c) supplies an anthropic argument tying near-criticality to observer existence (Buttazzo et al. multiverse arguments). Route (b) is currently most concrete: if the Higgs is the inflaton with a non-minimal curvature coupling, the inflationary plateau requires lambda(M_Pl) near zero, which is what we observe within current uncertainties.
scale_hint: Planck scale (10^19 GeV). The stability boundary is set by lambda(M_Pl); the closing mechanism must operate at or near M_Pl. Note that susy_scalar_partners is excluded because SUSY removes the metastability puzzle entirely — squark/slepton contributions keep lambda positive up to M_Pl, but at the cost of decoupling from this gap.

principles_at_stake.note: Near-criticality is itself either a fine-tuning or a structural prediction; the closing mechanism's stance on naturalness determines which.

literature_anchors.citation: Buttazzo, Degrassi, Giardino, Giudice, Sala, Salvio, Strumia, 'Investigating the near-criticality of the Higgs boson', JHEP 12 (2013) 089
Bezrukov and Shaposhnikov, 'The Standard Model Higgs boson as the inflaton', PLB 659, 703 (2008)
Bednyakov, Kniehl, Pikelner, Veretin, 'Stability of the electroweak vacuum: gauge independence and advanced precision', PRL 115, 201802 (2015)
Khoury and Steinhardt, 'Coincident inflation and the Higgs metastability puzzle' (2019)
